headline: Schema-valid extraction is not evidence fidelity. A naive structured-output baseline frequently produced valid-looking but ungrounded quotations; candidate-first extraction was built to make that failure visible and reduce it through deterministic provenance.

heldout_engineering_run.how_to_read_this: The low provenance pass rate is the baseline finding, not a defect in the repository. It quantifies how often schema-valid output was not exactly grounded in the source text.

candidate_first_v4_final_rerun.interpretation: strongest current engineering architecture; over-extraction unresolved

candidate_first_v4_stability.interpretation: passed deterministic gates, but model-selected ambiguous candidates are not perfectly repeatable

candidate_first_v4_source_fidelity_audit.interpretation: deterministic extractive summaries removed unsupported numeric-detail leakage; remaining issues require factual review

claims_boundary: Aggregate engineering and proxy evidence only; no clinical accuracy, safety, appropriateness, harmfulness, or generalizability claim.
